endUserId.title: Enduserid

sessionId.description: Continue an existing conversation. Omit to start a new one; the response returns the sessionId to send on follow-up turns.
sessionId.title: Sessionid

citations.description: documentId values the answer relies on
citations.title: Citations

confident.description: False when the data could not support a reliable answer
confident.title: Confident

sessionId.description: Conversation id — send back to continue this chat

accuracy.description: tp / total_expected — 'correct out of all expected'
accuracy.title: Accuracy

CreditMeter.description:
Discriminates which balance a credit transaction debits/credits.

``processing_credits`` covers both subscription and enterprise ledgers
(distinguished by ``subscription_id IS NULL`` on enterprise rows).
``filetag_credits`` covers the per-user FileTag allowance stored in
``filetag_credit_balances``. Separate meters so reports and history
views can filter cleanly without conflating the two accounting pools.
CreditMeter.title: CreditMeter

CreditMeterFilter.description:
Query-param filter for credit history endpoints.

Superset of :class:`CreditMeter` with an ``all`` sentinel for admin and
unified-history views that want both meters interleaved. Read endpoints
default to ``processing_credits`` so pre-audit-trail behavior is
preserved — mixing meters in a single page confuses ``balance_after``
readings since each meter has its own running balance.
CreditMeterFilter.title: CreditMeterFilter

total.description: Total number of transactions matching the filter, across all pages. Clients can use this to render 'Page X of Y' without a second count call. Scoped to the same ``meter`` filter as ``transactions`` when one is applied.
total.title: Total

ExtractionTypeModel.description: Defines the type of operation to perform on the uploaded document
ExtractionTypeModel.title: ExtractionTypeModel

meter.description: Which credit pool paid for the extraction. 'filetag_credits' for FileTag extractions, 'processing_credits' for subscription / trial / enterprise extractions. None when cost lookup was skipped.

FileTagBalanceOutDTO.description: Current user's FileTag balance + allowance for the dashboard surface.
FileTagBalanceOutDTO.title: FileTagBalanceOutDTO

FileTagFilenamePatternsOutDTO.description:
Suggested filename patterns produced by ``generate_filename_patterns``.

Six variants are surfaced so the caller can pick the layout that best
matches their downstream filing system. Long forms (``*_full``) preserve
the full extracted metadata; short forms truncate to a sensible length.
FileTagFilenamePatternsOutDTO.title: FileTagFilenamePatternsOutDTO

FileTagMetadataOutDTO.description:
Flat, document-type-agnostic metadata extracted by FileTag.

Mirrors :class:`app.components.documents.processors.filetag.models.FileTagFieldsModel`
but surfaces plain scalar values (unwrapped from the ``{value, coordinates}``
storage shape) so callers don't need to understand the extraction wire
format.
FileTagMetadataOutDTO.title: FileTagMetadataOutDTO

FileTagResultOutDTO.description: Final FileTagService result -- returned by the REST router and the MCP tool.

enriched_file_expires_at.description: Absolute UTC timestamp when ``enriched_file_url`` stops resolving.
enriched_file_expires_at.title: Enriched File Expires At

enriched_file_expires_in_seconds.description: Seconds until ``enriched_file_url`` expires, captured at response time. Easier for agents to reason about than the absolute timestamp.
enriched_file_expires_in_seconds.title: Enriched File Expires In Seconds

enriched_file_url.description: Short-lived signed URL to the input file with the extracted metadata embedded directly into the document properties (XMP for PDFs, EXIF/XMP for images). This is the canonical enriched output of the tool -- the file the user actually wants. Agents should download it within ``enriched_file_expires_in_seconds`` and save it under ``suggested_filename`` rather than embedding metadata client-side. Skip the download only if the user explicitly asked for the metadata payload alone.
enriched_file_url.title: Enriched File Url

next_action.description: Structured copy-paste recipe for the post-tag follow-up: HTTP GET ``enriched_file_url`` and save as ``suggested_filename``. Mirrors the ``next_tool_call`` pattern from ``files_create_upload`` -- one structured instruction per step keeps multi-step flows reliable across volatile agent scratchpads.

suggested_filename.description: Recommended filename for the enriched file -- one of the six ``filename_patterns``, picked as the safest default for general use. Pair this with ``enriched_file_url`` when saving the downloaded file.
suggested_filename.title: Suggested Filename

FileTagResultOutDTO.title: FileTagResultOutDTO

instruction.default: Once the PUT upload returns 200/204, call the tool above with the arguments above to run the FileTag pipeline on the uploaded file.
instruction.title: Instruction

GenerateTemplateFromUrlInDTO.description: DTO for generating template from a URL.
GenerateTemplateFromUrlInDTO.title: GenerateTemplateFromUrlInDTO

NextActionOutDTO.description:
Structured follow-up recipe for an agent capability (not an MCP tool).

Sister pattern to :class:`app.components.files.dtos.FilesNextToolCallOutDTO`:
that one names a specific MCP tool by literal name (``tag_file``); this
one names a generic *capability* (``http_get_and_save``) the agent maps
to whatever local mechanism it has -- ``WebFetch``, ``curl`` via shell,
a filesystem write tool, etc. We can't bind a specific tool name here
because the right one varies per agent host (Claude / GPT / OpenClaw /
raw HTTP client all differ).

Keeping the field structured (not just a description) defeats the
"scratchpad volatility" failure mode where an agent forgets to act on
the enriched-file URL after the metadata response satisfies the
immediate ask.

instruction.default: HTTP GET the URL and save the response bytes to disk as ``save_as``. The file is the original upload with the extracted metadata embedded into its document properties (XMP for PDFs, EXIF/XMP for images) -- no client-side embedding needed. Skip only if the user explicitly asked for the metadata payload alone.
instruction.title: Instruction

NextActionOutDTO.title: NextActionOutDTO

purgedIds.description: List of IDs of the purged documents
purgedIds.title: Purgedids

matchedChunks.description: Line-item/section snippets that matched the query (chunk search)
matchedChunks.title: Matchedchunks

count.description: documents in which this field appears
count.title: Count

RetrievalFieldsOutDTO.description:
Observed structured field NAMES per document type (self-query catalog).

Names only — never values. Same envelope (servedAt) as the sibling
retrieval endpoints.
RetrievalFieldsOutDTO.title: RetrievalFieldsOutDTO

RetrievalFiltersDTO.description:
Structured filters over promoted index columns.

Tenant scope is NOT part of this DTO by design: ``user_id`` always comes
from the authenticated context, server-side. ``end_user_id`` is a trusted
within-tenant filter on the server-to-server path (PRD §6.6).
RetrievalFiltersDTO.title: RetrievalFiltersDTO

structuredFilters.description: Self-query conditions over any structured field (whitelisted ops)
structuredFilters.title: Structuredfilters

RetrievalStatusOutDTO.description: Per-tenant snapshot of the document index.
RetrievalStatusOutDTO.title: RetrievalStatusOutDTO

endUserId.description: Optional end-user scope: the token will only see documents tagged to this end-user. Omit for an admin (whole-account) session.
endUserId.title: Enduserid

ttlSeconds.description: Requested TTL; clamped server-side to [300, 900] seconds.
ttlSeconds.title: Ttlseconds

StorageUsageOutDTO.description:
Tenant storage usage (FileTag-excluded). ``billingMode`` is
credits | money | none; ``unit`` is credits | usd. ``ratePerGbMonth`` and
``projectedCharge`` are 0 when storage is not billed (``none``).
StorageUsageOutDTO.title: StorageUsageOutDTO

StructuredFilterDTO.description:
One self-query condition over the ``structured`` JSONB (design §5, D6).

Deliberately narrow: TOP-LEVEL snake_case keys only, a closed op set,
values compiled to bind parameters — the model never writes SQL.
StructuredFilterDTO.title: StructuredFilterDTO

contentUrl.description: URL to the TOS page on the website
contentUrl.title: Contenturl

TagFileBodyDTO.description:
Body for ``POST /api/v1/files/{file_id}/tag`` -- file_id comes from the path.

Same shape as :class:`TagFileInDTO` minus ``file_id`` because the URL
path provides it. The router constructs a full ``TagFileInDTO`` from
``(path_file_id, body.external_id, body.end_user_id)`` before handing
off to :meth:`FilesService.tag_file`.
TagFileBodyDTO.title: TagFileBodyDTO

info.description: Extract structured data from invoices and financial documents, then search, aggregate, and chat over everything you've processed. Authenticate with your API key in the `X-API-Key` header; browser-embedded components use short-lived session tokens minted via `POST /v1/sessions/token`. Guides: https://console.gemina.co/docs
info.title: Gemina API

post.description:
Ask a natural-language question about the tenant's indexed documents.

Returns a grounded answer with documentId citations; ``confident=false``
means the underlying data could not support a reliable answer. Send back
the returned ``sessionId`` to continue the conversation; omit it to start
a new one.

delete.description:
End a conversation explicitly ('new chat' hygiene).

404 for unknown, expired, or foreign sessions alike — ids can't be probed.

post.description:
Fetch a remote URL server-side under SSRF guards, then run FileTag.

The bytes never traverse the LLM context -- the URL goes in, the
server fetches via :func:`fetch_safe`, and the result-DTO comes back.
Token cost is independent of file size on this path.

Reserve a pre-signed PUT slot for a forthcoming upload.

Returns the signed URL, the headers the agent must echo on the PUT,
the slot expiry, and a ``next_tool_call`` recipe pointing at
``tag_file`` -- the agent copy-pastes the ``file_id`` to run the
pipeline against the uploaded bytes.

Run the FileTag pipeline against a previously uploaded slot.

``file_id`` is taken from the URL path; the optional ``external_id``
and ``end_user_id`` come from the JSON body. The router constructs a
:class:`TagFileInDTO` from the path + body and hands it off to the
service.

Extract filing metadata (title, type, date, tags, ...) from a single
PDF or image and return a suggested filename plus a set of filename
patterns.

Requires API-key auth (``X-API-Key`` header). Subject to the per-API-key
burst rate limit and -- for free-tier users -- the monthly FileTag
credit allowance.

get.description:
Current user's FileTag balance, effective monthly allowance, and next
reset timestamp. Used by the console dashboard to surface remaining tags.

Paginated audit trail of FileTag credit transactions for the current
user. Returns the same rows as
``GET /v1/subscriptions/me/credits?meter=filetag_credits`` — kept as a
FileTag-namespaced alias so free-tier users (who have no subscription
endpoint affinity) can discover the history endpoint naturally.

post.description:
Browser-safe variant of ``POST /v1/filetag``.

Authenticates with the caller's JWT (``Authorization: Bearer <jwt>``)
and treats the ``X-API-Key`` header as the *UUID id* of one of the
user's API keys — NOT the raw secret. The key's ownership is verified
server-side by matching ``APIKeyModel.id == header AND user_id ==
jwt``, so the raw secret never needs to leave the server.

Mirrors the auth pattern used by ``POST /v1/documents/uploads/user``.
For server-to-server callers (the raw-key path used by MCP and public
SDKs), continue to use ``POST /v1/filetag``.

get.description:
Returns the public free-tier configuration (FileTag plan) used by the
console pricing page, MCP landing page, etc. Requires an authenticated
caller but is otherwise read-only and safe for USER, APP, and ADMIN.

Returns the current Terms of Service version, effective date,
content (markdown), and website URL.

post.description:
Sums/averages/counts/group-bys over the tenant's document index.

Deterministic DSL compiled server-side against whitelisted columns.
When money metrics are requested without a currency filter/grouping, the
server groups by currency automatically (mixed-currency sums are wrong by
construction) and flags it in ``meta.currencyGroupingAdded``. Same dual
auth as /query (API key, or Bearer token with enforced end-user scope).

get.description:
Observed structured field NAMES per document type (self-query catalog).

Names only — no values; tenant-scoped like every retrieval path.

post.description:
Structured filters, semantic or hybrid search over the tenant's index.

Auth: X-API-Key (server-to-server; ``filters.endUserId`` is a trusted
filter) OR a Bearer retrieval token (embedded SDK; a token-pinned
end-user scope is ENFORCED server-side and cannot be widened by the
request body). The tenant boundary always comes from the credential.

get.description:
How many of the tenant's documents are currently indexed.

Same dual auth as /query: X-API-Key OR a Bearer retrieval token — but
only an *unpinned* (whole-account) token is accepted. An end-user-pinned
token gets 403: the account-wide index size must not leak into a single
end-user's browser session.

post.description:
Mint a short-lived, signed, query-only retrieval token.

Scope is pinned server-side from the authenticated key: the tenant is the
key's user; an optional ``endUserId`` narrows the session to one end-user.

Browser-safe variant of ``POST /v1/sessions/token`` for logged-in users.

Authenticates with the caller's JWT (``Authorization: Bearer <jwt>``)
and treats the ``X-API-Key`` header as the *UUID id* of one of the
user's API keys — NOT the raw secret. Ownership is verified server-side
(``APIKeyModel.id == header AND user_id == jwt``), so the raw secret
never leaves the server. Mirrors ``POST /v1/filetag/user``. Deliberately,
the key's IP allowlist is NOT enforced on this path: the console runs
from arbitrary user IPs, and JWT auth + server-side ownership is the
control — the same accepted convention as ``/v1/filetag/user``.

The minted token is identical to the server-to-server variant: signed,
query-only, TTL-clamped, tenant pinned from the key.

parameters.description: Meter to page. Defaults to 'processing_credits' so each page has a single running balance. Use 'filetag_credits' for the FileTag audit trail, or 'all' for an interleaved admin view (balance_after will jump between pools — expect to render a meter column).
parameters.name: meter

schema.description: Meter to page. Defaults to 'processing_credits' so each page has a single running balance. Use 'filetag_credits' for the FileTag audit trail, or 'all' for an interleaved admin view (balance_after will jump between pools — expect to render a meter column).

get.description: List all templates for the authenticated user.

post.description: Create a new extraction template with a provided schema.

get.description: List all templates available for the user associated with the API key.

post.description: Upload a PDF or image to generate a template. Processes synchronously (up to 60s).
Upload a PDF or image to generate a template. Returns immediately with template in PENDING status.
Provide a URL to generate a template. Returns immediately with template in PENDING status.
Provide a URL to a PDF or image to generate a template. Processes synchronously (up to 60s).

delete.description: Soft delete a template and clean up associated storage.

get.description: Get a specific template by ID.

post.description: Activate a frozen template. Requires available capacity within the plan's template limit.
Deactivate (freeze) a template. Frozen templates cannot be used in extractions or edited.
